rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees referring to ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope rules: component type contracts use component ports, implementation contracts use subcomponent ports and bindings.
Only reference visible AADL features or helpers declared in the same annex block.
Do not invent numeric bounds or event port encodings unless explicitly stated in the requirement or model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only data types and identifiers present in the AADL model's scope.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not encode AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE expressions unless model exposes typed ports.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator diagnostics
